title: Tempting Crisp Korean Cobnuts Hwajeon
description: A sweet and crisp Korean dish featuring cobnuts, perfect for a brunch mood, made with a tagine cooking method and kosher friendly ingredients, in the form of hwajeon.
instructions: Preheat the tagine or a large skillet with a heat diffuser over medium heat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and sesame oil.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Heat the vegetable oil in the preheated tagine or skillet.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter into the hot oil.
Flatten the batter slightly with a spatula and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, until crispy and golden brown.
Serve the hwajeon hot and enjoy.
